critical_issues: Discrepancy in customer's last name (Aptalakar vs Atyalkar) between transcript and MER.
Discrepancy in PAN card number between transcript and MER.

Customer provided inconsistent 'No' answers to general medical history questions (Last5Years.a, b, c) which contradict previously disclosed conditions (fistula surgery, piles, related tests/treatment/absence from work). The doctor did not adequately clarify these inconsistencies.
recommendations: The doctor should ensure all personal particulars, especially name and ID details, are accurately captured and verified against the MER.
The doctor should cross-verify customer's 'No' answers to general medical history questions against previously disclosed conditions to ensure consistency and clarify any discrepancies.
The doctor should explicitly ask for details regarding 'Piles' if it's a listed condition, even if the MER template doesn't provide specific sub-questions for it, to ensure complete disclosure.
The doctor should be mindful of potential third-party influence when the customer is observed interacting with others during the call.

customer_satisfaction_indicators: Customer is cooperative and answers questions.

validation_errors: Customer's 'No' to 'Last5Years.a' (medical tests) contradicts prior disclosure of tests for piles.
Customer's 'No' to 'Last5Years.b' (medication/treatment > 2 weeks, specialist, hospitalization) contradicts prior disclosure of fistula surgery.
Customer's 'No' to 'Last5Years.c' (inability to work > 2 weeks) contradicts prior disclosure of inability to work for fistula.
